title: Apparatus and method for treatment of dry eyes
abstract: A disposable/removable tip attachable on a distal end of a fiber bundle, allowing transmitting light energy from the fiber bundle to an eyelid and comprising a contact surface made of bio-compatible material, which allows to contact the eyelid and in addition an add-on device, comprising a fiber bundle, having a distal and proximal end, wherein a disposable/removable tip is attachable to the distal end, while the proximal end is connected to an optical coupler, designed for optical and/or energy coupling with a lightguide of a Meibomian gland expression (MGX) treatment device.
claims:
Claims WHAT WE CLAIM IS: 1. A disposable/removable tip configured to: - being attachable on the distal end of a fiber bundle; - allowing transmitting light energy from the fiber bundle to an eyelid; and comprising : - a contact surface made of bio-compatible material, which allows to contact the eyelid. 2. The tip of claim 1, characterized in that, the tip is made of transparent material, such as silicone. 3. The tip of one of the previous claims, characterized in that, the tip is made of semi-transparent material, such as doped or tinted material, such as silicone. 4. The tip of one of the previous claims, characterized in that, the tip is provided packed in a clean/sterile state. 5. The tip of one of the previous claims, characterized in that, a round shaped, soft tip adapted to form a contact surface between the tip and an eyelid of a patient. 6. The tip of one of the previous claims, characterized in that, the contact surface is concave shaped and able to function like a vacuum cup. 7. The tip of claim 6, characterized in that, the tip is attachable to a tube or shaft, which surrounds 

 the fiber bundle. 8. The tip of claim 7, characterized in that, a radius of the concave shaped contact surface changes, when the fiber bundle is moved relatively to the tube or shaft . 9. The tip of one of the previous claims, characterized in that, the tip includes an proximal tip and distal tip , which form a clamp adapted to encompass or grasp the eyelid therebetween . 10. The tip of claim 9, characterized in that, the distance between the proximal tip ( and the distal tip is variable allowing to squeeze the eyelid. 11. The tip of one of the claims 9 or 10, characterized in that, the proximal tip comprises an opaque plastic . 12. The tip of one of the claims 9 to 11, characterized in that, the outer surface of the tip or the distal tip is coated with a reflective coating/layer . 13. The tip of one of the claims 10 to 12, characterized in that a telescope or a sledge mechanic is able to vary the distance between the proximal tip and the distal tip. 14. An add-on device, comprising: - a fiber bundle, having a distal end and a proximal end ; - wherein a tip according to one of the previous claims is 

 attachable to the distal end; - the proximal end being connected to an optical coupler , designed for optical and/or energy coupling with a lightguide of an IPL device or other light source. 15. The add-on device of claim 14, characterized in that, close to the distal end the fiber bundle is attached to a handle adapter , where a handle can be attached. 16. The add-on device of one of the claims 14 or 15, characterized in that, the fiber bundle incorporates a filter for wavelength selection and/or damping. 17. The add-on device of one of the claims 14 to 16, characterized in that, parts of the add-on device are designed to degrade, when cleaned or sterilized. 18. System for performing IPL/ light treatment of a patients eyelids, comprising: - an add-on device according to one of claims 14 to 17; and - a tip according to one of the claims 1 to 13, whereby the tip is attached to the distal end of the fiber bundle. 19. Method for treating blepharitis or Meibomian gland dysfunction comprising the steps of Treating a patients eyelids with IPL/ light using a tip according to any of the claims 1 to 13, Squeezing the patient's eyelids in order to conduct Meibomian gland expression (MGX) . 20. Method for treating blepharitis according to claim 19 whereby the eyelid is first heated a predetermined time or until the temperature of a patient's eyelid exceeds a predetermined threshold and then MGX is executed. 21. Method for treating blepharitis according to claim 19 or 20 whereby the steps of heating/ providing IPL/ light and exerting pressure on the eyelid are executed simultaneously or intermittently
